subject: DevOps
title: DNS in the Cloud
description: A quick win: understand DNS in the Cloud.

hook.text: Users type example.com and reach 52.14.198.73. DNS translates human names into machine addresses.

buildup.text: Route 53, Cloud DNS, and Azure DNS host records with global, low-latency name resolution.

discovery.text: DNS routing policies: weighted splits traffic, latency picks the fastest region, failover heals.

twist.text: DNS-based traffic management replaces hardware routers for global steering to healthy regions.

climax.text: Low TTLs enable fast failover. A 60-second TTL means clients find new IPs within one minute.

punchline.text: Cloud DNS routes, balances, and heals — not just names.

quiz.question: What does latency-based DNS routing do?
options: Directs users to the cloud region with the lowest latency for them
Reduces the DNS query time to zero
Only resolves DNS for low-latency connections
